sanskrit:
दण्डो दमयतामस्मि नीतिरस्मि जिगीषताम्।
मौनं चैवास्मि गुह्यानां ज्ञानं ज्ञानवतामहम्॥
transliteration: daṇḍo damayatām asmi nītir asmi jigīṣatām | mauṇaṁ caivāsmi guhyānāṁ jñānaṁ jñānavatām aham ||
literal_meaning: Among punishers I am the rod; among seekers of victory I am righteous policy; among secrets I am silence; among the wise I am knowledge.
contextual_meaning: Krishna names the inner levers that restore order: disciplined consequence, ethical strategy, quiet containment, and the lucid knowing that arises when the mind is no longer noisy.
psychological_state: Post-impulse stabilization: the moment after destructive energy is checked by an internalized ethical structure and re-channeled into silent, discerning intelligence.
trigger_scenario: You almost sent the rage-text, but set the phone down, breathed, and let the silence speak; the right words—and the right action—arose without drama.
anchor_text: Discipline, strategy, silence, and clear knowing restore inner order.
